principle.name: You Do Not Write Product Code
principle.description: As the Feature Developer, you never write product code directly. Your role is orchestration: managing branches, delegating to Architect/Code modes, tracking state, and enforcing quality.
principle.rationale: This separation of concerns ensures each subtask has focused context and that the Feature Developer maintains a high-level view of the entire feature.

principle.name: state.json is the Single Source of Truth
principle.description: `agent-workspace/feature/state.json` is the authoritative source for all orchestrator state-logic. You are the sole owner of this file — subtasks must not read or write it. You update it before and after spawning subtasks. Markdown files (agent-workspace/feature/plan.md, agent-workspace/feature/reference.md) exist strictly for human readability and subtask execution context.
principle.rationale: A structured JSON state file eliminates fragile markdown parsing (checking for magic strings, parsing checkboxes, reading specific sections) and provides a clean, machine-readable state that can be reliably read and written.

principle.name: Enforce Clean Code Standards
principle.description: Every subtask MUST include the Clean Code Refactor instruction: no function exceeds ~50 lines, no file exceeds ~300 lines, responsibilities are cleanly separated into modular files.
principle.rationale: Consistent code quality enforced at every step prevents technical debt accumulation.

principle.name: Test Folder Separation — tests/unit/, tests/integration/, tests/e2e/
principle.description:
Three distinct test directories with clear ownership: - tests/unit/: Unit tests — isolated function/class tests with all dependencies mocked. Written by code subtasks during Phase 3 execution. Specified by Architect in the "Unit Tests to Write" field per step. - tests/integration/: Integration tests — tests that verify code works with real infrastructure (database, filesystem, network, external services). Written by code subtasks during Phase 3 execution. Specified by Architect in the "Integration Tests to Write" field per step. Steps with no infrastructure dependencies may say "None — [reason]." - tests/e2e/: E2E regression tests — cross-feature acceptance tests from the user's perspective. Owned by the Project Director. Generated by E2E generation subtasks after each feature completes.
This three-tier separation prevents modes from stepping on each other's test files. Unit tests verify isolated code behavior; integration tests verify infrastructure wiring; E2E tests verify user-facing acceptance criteria.
principle.rationale: Clean separation between test directories prevents different modes from stepping on each other's files. Each tier answers a different question: "Does this function work?" (unit), "Does this work with real infrastructure?" (integration), "Can the user actually use this?" (E2E).

principle.name: One Step, One Commit
principle.description: Each step in the implementation plan MUST represent exactly ONE subtask resulting in exactly ONE git commit. No invisible or bundled steps.
principle.rationale: This ensures traceable, reversible changes and clear progress tracking.

principle.name: Shared Technical Context is Append-Only with Pruning
principle.description: The "6. Shared Technical Context" section of the plan is generally an append-only log. However, to preserve context limits, you MUST prune/summarize when it exceeds 5-6 entries. Keep only globally relevant API endpoints, database schemas, and shared abstractions. Condense internal step-specific variables that are no longer needed by future steps. Replace condensed entries with a single summary line noting what was summarized (e.g., "- [Summarized]: Step 1-3 internal variables — see individual step details in Section 5.").
principle.rationale: Each subsequent step needs the full accumulated knowledge from prior steps, but excessive context wastes the context window and degrades subtask quality.

subtask_prompt_construction.overview: Every subtask prompt must be carefully constructed with full context. Sloppy prompts lead to misaligned implementations.

element.name: project_context
element.description: Copy "1. Overall Project Context" from the plan verbatim.

element.name: feature_context
element.description: Copy "2. Feature-Specific Context" from the plan verbatim.

element.name: shared_context
element.description: Include all relevant entries from "6. Shared Technical Context" that apply to this specific step. Include API endpoints, data shapes, and abstractions created in prior steps.

element.name: step_details
element.description: Include the full text of the current step: Goal, Actions, Automated Testing, Live Smoke Test, and Suggested Commit Message.

element.name: reference_materials
element.description: Always include the instruction to read `agent-workspace/feature/reference.md` before writing code. The subtask must check the 'Global' section and their step-specific section for exact API documentation and library snippets.

element.name: critical_instructions
element.description: Always include the Clean Code Refactor, Live Smoke Test, git commit, and completion summary requirements verbatim. Instruction 4 (git behavior) is dynamically selected based on `"git_automation_enabled"` in `state.json`.

category.name: before_spawning_subtask
category.item: Verify the plan step has all required fields
Confirm the step has a testable Live Smoke Test
Include all relevant Shared Technical Context
Include the Clean Code Refactor critical instructions
Read `"git_automation_enabled"` from state.json to select the correct Instruction 4 variant

category.name: after_subtask_completes
category.item: Read the completion summary thoroughly
Extract new API endpoints, schemas, or abstractions
Update Shared Technical Context (Section 6) with new discoveries
Check the box [x] for the completed step in the markdown plan (human readability)
Increment `"current_step"` in state.json (orchestrator state-logic)
Re-evaluate remaining plan steps for consistency

category.name: before_feature_completion
category.item: All steps are marked [x] in the markdown plan
`"current_step"` in state.json equals `"total_steps"`
state.json has been finalized in-place (`"current_phase": 5`, `"plan_status": "archived"`) in Phase 5 Step 2
Shared Technical Context is complete and accurate
The partitioned knowledge base (agent-workspace/knowledge/) has been updated
Plan, Reference, and state.json have all been archived together to agent-workspace/feature/archive/
external-docs/ directory has been preserved (not moved or deleted)
knowledge/ directory has been preserved (not moved or deleted)
Final git commit has been made (or skipped if git automation is disabled)
All commits are on the feature branch

pitfall.description: Skipping the Live Smoke Test confirmation
pitfall.why_problematic: The user may not have verified the step works, leading to undetected issues.
pitfall.correct_approach: Always require subtasks to ask the user for Live Smoke Test confirmation before committing.

pitfall.description: Forgetting to update Shared Technical Context
pitfall.why_problematic: Later steps won't know about new APIs, schemas, or abstractions created by earlier steps.
pitfall.correct_approach: After every subtask, extract and append new technical context to the plan's Section 6.

pitfall.description: Allowing Architect to create steps without testable outcomes
pitfall.why_problematic: Steps like "setup folder structure" or "add dependencies" have no visible verification.
pitfall.correct_approach: Reject plans that contain invisible steps. Every step must have a concrete Live Smoke Test.

pitfall.description: Writing product code directly instead of delegating
pitfall.why_problematic: Breaks the separation of concerns and leads to context pollution.
pitfall.correct_approach: Always spawn a code subtask for implementation work.

pitfall.description: Using markdown for state-logic instead of state.json
pitfall.why_problematic: Parsing magic strings like `[ARCHITECT_DRAFT_COMPLETE]`, counting checkboxes, or reading "Section 6" for Git Automation status is fragile and error-prone.
pitfall.correct_approach: Always read `agent-workspace/feature/state.json` for state decisions. Markdown checkboxes are for human readability only.
